areas.area: Method and code fidelity to the paper

leaves.label: The Adam optimizer implementation contains the exact update equations of Algorithm 1 (Section 2): m_t = β1·m_{t-1} + (1-β1)·g_t, v_t = β2·v_
leaves.why: provenance.json (mnist_logreg.per_optimizer.adam: lr=0.001, betas=[0.9,0.999], eps=1e-8) and code/optimizers.py's AdamNoBiasCorrection (m.mul_(beta1).add_(grad,...), v.mul_(beta2).addcmul_(grad,grad,...) with zeros_like init) confirm the Algorithm-1 recursion family ran, but the

leaves.label: The Adam implementation exposes and defaults to the paper's recommended hyperparameters from Algorithm 1 (Section 2): α = 0.001, β1 = 0.9, β
leaves.why: provenance.json experiments.mnist_logreg.per_optimizer.adam records exactly lr=0.001, betas=[0.9, 0.999], eps=1e-08, matching the paper's Algorithm 1 defaults.

leaves.label: The AdaMax variant of Algorithm 2 (Section 7.1) is implemented with the infinity-norm recursion u_t = max(β2·u_{t-1}, |g_t|), update θ_t = θ
leaves.why: code/optimizers.py AdaMax.step implements u.mul_(beta2); torch.maximum(u, grad.abs(), out=u) (no bias correction on u) with step_size = lr/(1-beta1**t) and defaults lr=0.002, betas=(0.9, 0.999), exactly Algorithm 2.

leaves.label: All comparison optimizers named in Section 6 are implemented: SGD with Nesterov momentum, AdaGrad with update θ_{t+1} = θ_t - α·g_t/√(Σ_{i=1
leaves.why: provenance.json records sgd_nesterov (momentum=0.9, nesterov=true), adagrad, rmsprop, and adadelta/adadelta_dropout (mnist_mlp.per_optimizer) all with measured final_train_loss values, and per-cell artifact dirs (e.g. outputs/.../mnist_mlp_dropout_adadelta, cifar10_dropout_rmspro

leaves.label: The Section 6.4 ablation variant is implemented as Adam with the bias-correction divisors (1-β1^t) and (1-β2^t) removed — equivalent to RMSP
leaves.why: code/optimizers.py AdamNoBiasCorrection is a separate Optimizer class identical to Adam with the (1-β1^t)/(1-β2^t) divisors removed (comment 'NO bias correction — use m_t, v_t directly'), and fig_fig4_vae_lr_sweep.json contains paired no-correction/bias-corrected series confirmin

leaves.label: The logistic regression experiment loads MNIST and classifies directly on the 784-dimension flattened image vectors with L2 regularization,
leaves.why: provenance.json mnist_logreg records weight_decay=0.0005 (L2 regularization) for the MNIST logistic-regression experiment, and metrics.json mnist_logreg test_accuracy ≈92.6% is exactly the signature of a linear classifier on flattened 784-d MNIST inputs.

leaves.label: The IMDB movie review dataset (Maas et al., 2011) is preprocessed into bag-of-words feature vectors over the 10,000 most frequent words, and
leaves.why: provenance.json experiments.imdb_bow records vocab_size=10000 and dropout=0.5, with measured per-optimizer convergence series confirming the BoW IMDB experiment actually ran.

leaves.label: The CIFAR-10 experiment applies whitening preprocessing to the input images and applies dropout noise to the input layer and the fully conne
leaves.why: Cell dirs outputs/prj_...-75b91ab0/cifar10_dropout_*/cifar10_nodropout_* show a dropout condition was attempted, but neither the manifest nor metrics.json evidence whitening or the dropout placement Section 6.3 specifies.

leaves.label: Executed run artifacts (logs plus per-epoch training-cost records) exist for the Section 6.1 logistic regression on MNIST and IMDB with mini
leaves.why: metrics.json holds per-epoch train_loss histories for mnist_logreg adam/sgd_nesterov/adagrad, provenance.json records batch_size=128 for both mnist_logreg and imdb_bow plus lr_schedule='1/sqrt(t)' for MNIST, and per-cell logs (mnist_logreg_adam.log, imdb_adagrad.log, etc.) exist.

leaves.label: Executed run artifacts exist for the Section 6.2 multi-layer neural network on MNIST — two fully connected hidden layers with 1000 hidden un
leaves.why: provenance.json experiments.mnist_mlp records batch_size=128 with measured final_train_loss and convergence series for both dropout (adagrad_dropout, rmsprop_dropout, adadelta_dropout, adam_dropout, sgd_momentum_dropout) and deterministic no-dropout variants, and matching cell di

leaves.label: Executed run artifacts exist for the Section 6.3 CIFAR-10 CNN with the c64-c64-c128-1000 architecture (three alternating stages of 5x5 convo
leaves.why: Per-cell dirs/logs (cifar10_dropout_adagrad, cifar10_nodropout_adam, cifar10_dropout_sgd_nesterov, etc.) show the runs were launched, but the empty fig3 sidecar series and absent cifar10 metrics/manifest entries leave no measured evidence of completed Section 6.3 runs.

leaves.label: Executed run artifacts exist for the Section 6.4 VAE bias-correction grid — single hidden layer of 500 softplus units and a 50-dimensional s
leaves.why: fig_fig4_vae_lr_sweep.json contains measured ELBO values for β1∈{0.0,0.9} × β2∈{0.99,0.999,0.9999} × {no-correction, bias-corrected} × {10, 100} epoch checkpoints across log10(α) ∈ [-5,-2], evidencing most of the Section 6.4 grid was executed.

leaves.label: Training cost is measured as the training negative log likelihood and plotted against 'iterations over entire dataset' (epochs), matching th
leaves.why: fig_fig1_mnist_logreg.json and fig_fig1_imdb_bow.json show y='training cost (NLL)' vs x='epoch' with full per-epoch series, and fig_fig2_mnist_mlp.json records axis.y.scale='log' matching Figure 2, though its series block is empty.

leaves.label: Per Section 6, all optimizers in a given experiment use the same parameter initialization, and hyper-parameters such as learning rate and mo
leaves.why: The only recorded hyperparameter sweep is the Section 6.4 LR grid in fig_fig4_vae_lr_sweep.json; identical init across optimizers and a dense grid with selected-best settings for the main experiments are absent from provenance.json and metrics.json.

leaves.label: The Section 6.4 evaluation plots loss (y-axis) versus log10(α) (x-axis) for each (β1, β2) combination, with separate curves/panels for bias-
leaves.why: fig_fig4_vae_lr_sweep.json plots against x='log10(α)' with separate measured series per (β1,β2) combination for bias-corrected vs no-correction at both _10 and _100 epoch checkpoints (e.g. 'b1=0.9_b2=0.99_no-correction_100'), matching Figure 4's protocol structure.

leaves.label: The CIFAR-10 evaluation produces both views from Figure 3: training cost over the first 3 epochs and training cost over the full 45 epochs,
leaves.why: CIFAR-10 cells were executed for 6 optimizers in both conditions (outputs/prj_6d41d2f09c026403-75b91ab0/cifar10_{dropout,nodropout}_*/metrics.json) but the required 3-epoch view is absent and the fig3 sidecars carry no curve data.

areas.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

leaves.label: On MNIST logistic regression (Figure 1 left, Section 6.1), the produced curves show Adam converging at a rate similar to SGD with Nesterov m
leaves.why: metrics.json per_model.mnist_logreg shows adam final_train_loss=0.2352 ≈ sgd_nesterov 0.2376, both well below adagrad 0.2603, and the provenance.json convergence sampled series show adagrad above adam/sgd at every sampled epoch — matching the paper's Figure 1-left trend.

leaves.label: On IMDB BoW logistic regression with 50% dropout (Figure 1 right, Section 6.1), the produced curves show Adam converging as fast as Adagrad,
leaves.why: provenance.json imdb_bow convergence/finals show Adam clearly beating SGDNesterov (0.250 vs 0.297) as claimed, but AdaGrad (0.318) lands below SGDNesterov, inverting half the claimed ordering.

leaves.label: On the MNIST multilayer network with dropout (Figure 2a, Section 6.2), the produced curves show Adam achieving better (lower) training cost
leaves.why: provenance.json experiments.mnist_mlp.per_optimizer measured finals contradict the Figure 2a claim: Adam-with-dropout ranks worse than AdaGrad, AdaDelta, and SGD-momentum, beating only RMSProp.

leaves.label: On the CIFAR-10 ConvNet over 45 epochs (Figure 3 right, Section 6.3), the produced curves show Adam and SGDNesterov eventually converging co
leaves.why: Execution is evidenced only by per-cell outputs (outputs/prj_6d41d2f09c026403-75b91ab0/cifar10_*_{adam,adagrad,sgd_nesterov}/metrics.json in the listing), but no measured CIFAR-10 number or curve is readable anywhere, so the claimed trend is unverifiable.

leaves.label: In the VAE bias-correction study (Figure 4, Section 6.4), the produced results show that β2 values close to 1 (especially β2 = 0.9999) witho
leaves.why: fig_fig4_vae_lr_sweep.json measured series support the instability claim — b2=0.9999_no-correction terminates at log10(α)=-4 (missing -3 point) and b2=0.999_no-correction_10 gives -140.80 at -3 vs -109.86 bias-corrected — but the universal-dominance claim does not hold at the sma

leaves.label: The repository contains runnable entry-point scripts covering all four Section 6 experiments (MNIST/IMDB logistic regression, MNIST multilay
leaves.why: code/optimizers.py implements AdaMax (Algorithm 2) and AdamNoBiasCorrection, and all four Section-6 experiment families demonstrably ran through the train_cell.py entry point (cell outputs for mnist_logreg_*, imdb_*, mnist_mlp_*, cifar10_* plus the populated fig_fig4 VAE sweep si

leaves.label: Generated figure files reproducing the curves of Figures 1, 2, 3, and 4 are saved as artifacts with per-curve optimizer labels (e.g. 'Adam',
leaves.why: PNG artifacts exist for Figures 1–4 (outputs/fix_run/fig_fig1..fig_fig4*.png) and fig1/fig4 sidecars carry labeled series (e.g. 'AdaGrad' in fig_fig1_mnist_logreg.json), but the Figure 2 and Figure 3 sidecars contain no series at all.

leaves.label: Provenance artifacts exist: a dependency specification (e.g. requirements.txt or environment file) pinning library versions, recorded random
leaves.why: provenance.json records seed=42, batch_size=128, hardware=cuda, torch 2.5.1+cu121, and full Adam hyperparameters (lr=0.001, betas=[0.9,0.999], eps=1e-08) for mnist_logreg, and per-cell logs plus config_used.json exist per run (outputs/prj_6d41d2f09c026403-75b91ab0/*.log, outputs/

leaf_scores.justification: provenance.json (mnist_logreg.per_optimizer.adam: lr=0.001, betas=[0.9,0.999], eps=1e-8) and code/optimizers.py's AdamNoBiasCorrection (m.mul_(beta1).add_(grad,...), v.mul_(beta2).addcmul_(grad,grad,...) with zeros_like init) confirm the Algorithm-1 recursion family ran, but the bias-corrected Adam implementation cannot be directly inspected.
provenance.json experiments.mnist_logreg.per_optimizer.adam records exactly lr=0.001, betas=[0.9, 0.999], eps=1e-08, matching the paper's Algorithm 1 defaults.

code/optimizers.py AdaMax.step implements u.mul_(beta2); torch.maximum(u, grad.abs(), out=u) (no bias correction on u) with step_size = lr/(1-beta1**t) and defaults lr=0.002, betas=(0.9, 0.999), exactly Algorithm 2.

provenance.json records sgd_nesterov (momentum=0.9, nesterov=true), adagrad, rmsprop, and adadelta/adadelta_dropout (mnist_mlp.per_optimizer) all with measured final_train_loss values, and per-cell artifact dirs (e.g. outputs/.../mnist_mlp_dropout_adadelta, cifar10_dropout_rmsprop) show each was usable as a drop-in optimizer across the experiment scripts.

code/optimizers.py AdamNoBiasCorrection is a separate Optimizer class identical to Adam with the (1-β1^t)/(1-β2^t) divisors removed (comment 'NO bias correction — use m_t, v_t directly'), and fig_fig4_vae_lr_sweep.json contains paired no-correction/bias-corrected series confirming both variants ran.

provenance.json mnist_logreg records weight_decay=0.0005 (L2 regularization) for the MNIST logistic-regression experiment, and metrics.json mnist_logreg test_accuracy ≈92.6% is exactly the signature of a linear classifier on flattened 784-d MNIST inputs.
provenance.json experiments.imdb_bow records vocab_size=10000 and dropout=0.5, with measured per-optimizer convergence series confirming the BoW IMDB experiment actually ran.

Cell dirs outputs/prj_...-75b91ab0/cifar10_dropout_*/cifar10_nodropout_* show a dropout condition was attempted, but neither the manifest nor metrics.json evidence whitening or the dropout placement Section 6.3 specifies.

metrics.json holds per-epoch train_loss histories for mnist_logreg adam/sgd_nesterov/adagrad, provenance.json records batch_size=128 for both mnist_logreg and imdb_bow plus lr_schedule='1/sqrt(t)' for MNIST, and per-cell logs (mnist_logreg_adam.log, imdb_adagrad.log, etc.) exist.

provenance.json experiments.mnist_mlp records batch_size=128 with measured final_train_loss and convergence series for both dropout (adagrad_dropout, rmsprop_dropout, adadelta_dropout, adam_dropout, sgd_momentum_dropout) and deterministic no-dropout variants, and matching cell dirs mnist_mlp_dropout_*/mnist_mlp_nodropout_* (incl. sgd_nesterov) exist.

Per-cell dirs/logs (cifar10_dropout_adagrad, cifar10_nodropout_adam, cifar10_dropout_sgd_nesterov, etc.) show the runs were launched, but the empty fig3 sidecar series and absent cifar10 metrics/manifest entries leave no measured evidence of completed Section 6.3 runs.

fig_fig4_vae_lr_sweep.json contains measured ELBO values for β1∈{0.0,0.9} × β2∈{0.99,0.999,0.9999} × {no-correction, bias-corrected} × {10, 100} epoch checkpoints across log10(α) ∈ [-5,-2], evidencing most of the Section 6.4 grid was executed.

fig_fig1_mnist_logreg.json and fig_fig1_imdb_bow.json show y='training cost (NLL)' vs x='epoch' with full per-epoch series, and fig_fig2_mnist_mlp.json records axis.y.scale='log' matching Figure 2, though its series block is empty.

The only recorded hyperparameter sweep is the Section 6.4 LR grid in fig_fig4_vae_lr_sweep.json; identical init across optimizers and a dense grid with selected-best settings for the main experiments are absent from provenance.json and metrics.json.

fig_fig4_vae_lr_sweep.json plots against x='log10(α)' with separate measured series per (β1,β2) combination for bias-corrected vs no-correction at both _10 and _100 epoch checkpoints (e.g. 'b1=0.9_b2=0.99_no-correction_100'), matching Figure 4's protocol structure.

CIFAR-10 cells were executed for 6 optimizers in both conditions (outputs/prj_6d41d2f09c026403-75b91ab0/cifar10_{dropout,nodropout}_*/metrics.json) but the required 3-epoch view is absent and the fig3 sidecars carry no curve data.

metrics.json per_model.mnist_logreg shows adam final_train_loss=0.2352 ≈ sgd_nesterov 0.2376, both well below adagrad 0.2603, and the provenance.json convergence sampled series show adagrad above adam/sgd at every sampled epoch — matching the paper's Figure 1-left trend.
provenance.json imdb_bow convergence/finals show Adam clearly beating SGDNesterov (0.250 vs 0.297) as claimed, but AdaGrad (0.318) lands below SGDNesterov, inverting half the claimed ordering.
provenance.json experiments.mnist_mlp.per_optimizer measured finals contradict the Figure 2a claim: Adam-with-dropout ranks worse than AdaGrad, AdaDelta, and SGD-momentum, beating only RMSProp.

Execution is evidenced only by per-cell outputs (outputs/prj_6d41d2f09c026403-75b91ab0/cifar10_*_{adam,adagrad,sgd_nesterov}/metrics.json in the listing), but no measured CIFAR-10 number or curve is readable anywhere, so the claimed trend is unverifiable.

fig_fig4_vae_lr_sweep.json measured series support the instability claim — b2=0.9999_no-correction terminates at log10(α)=-4 (missing -3 point) and b2=0.999_no-correction_10 gives -140.80 at -3 vs -109.86 bias-corrected — but the universal-dominance claim does not hold at the smallest learning rates.

code/optimizers.py implements AdaMax (Algorithm 2) and AdamNoBiasCorrection, and all four Section-6 experiment families demonstrably ran through the train_cell.py entry point (cell outputs for mnist_logreg_*, imdb_*, mnist_mlp_*, cifar10_* plus the populated fig_fig4 VAE sweep sidecar).

PNG artifacts exist for Figures 1–4 (outputs/fix_run/fig_fig1..fig_fig4*.png) and fig1/fig4 sidecars carry labeled series (e.g. 'AdaGrad' in fig_fig1_mnist_logreg.json), but the Figure 2 and Figure 3 sidecars contain no series at all.

provenance.json records seed=42, batch_size=128, hardware=cuda, torch 2.5.1+cu121, and full Adam hyperparameters (lr=0.001, betas=[0.9,0.999], eps=1e-08) for mnist_logreg, and per-cell logs plus config_used.json exist per run (outputs/prj_6d41d2f09c026403-75b91ab0/*.log, outputs/fix_run/config_used.json), but a pinned dependency file is not evidenced.

weak_leaves.justification: provenance.json experiments.mnist_mlp.per_optimizer measured finals contradict the Figure 2a claim: Adam-with-dropout ranks worse than AdaGrad, AdaDelta, and SGD-momentum, beating only RMSProp.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: Execution is evidenced only by per-cell outputs (outputs/prj_6d41d2f09c026403-75b91ab0/cifar10_*_{adam,adagrad,sgd_nesterov}/metrics.json in the listing), but no measured CIFAR-10 number or curve is readable anywhere, so the claimed trend is unverifiable.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: Cell dirs outputs/prj_...-75b91ab0/cifar10_dropout_*/cifar10_nodropout_* show a dropout condition was attempted, but neither the manifest nor metrics.json evidence whitening or the dropout placement Section 6.3 specifies.

Per-cell dirs/logs (cifar10_dropout_adagrad, cifar10_nodropout_adam, cifar10_dropout_sgd_nesterov, etc.) show the runs were launched, but the empty fig3 sidecar series and absent cifar10 metrics/manifest entries leave no measured evidence of completed Section 6.3 runs.

The only recorded hyperparameter sweep is the Section 6.4 LR grid in fig_fig4_vae_lr_sweep.json; identical init across optimizers and a dense grid with selected-best settings for the main experiments are absent from provenance.json and metrics.json.

CIFAR-10 cells were executed for 6 optimizers in both conditions (outputs/prj_6d41d2f09c026403-75b91ab0/cifar10_{dropout,nodropout}_*/metrics.json) but the required 3-epoch view is absent and the fig3 sidecars carry no curve data.
provenance.json imdb_bow convergence/finals show Adam clearly beating SGDNesterov (0.250 vs 0.297) as claimed, but AdaGrad (0.318) lands below SGDNesterov, inverting half the claimed ordering.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: PNG artifacts exist for Figures 1–4 (outputs/fix_run/fig_fig1..fig_fig4*.png) and fig1/fig4 sidecars carry labeled series (e.g. 'AdaGrad' in fig_fig1_mnist_logreg.json), but the Figure 2 and Figure 3 sidecars contain no series at all.
